projectDescription: An open-source, desktop-scale dual-wheeled-leg balancing robot based on ESP32, using SimpleFOC for brushless motor control, LQR for self-balancing, and WebSocket-based WiFi remote control.
keyFeatures: ESP32-based control with WiFi (AP and STA modes)
SimpleFOC field-oriented control for brushless wheel motors
LQR self-balancing algorithm with adaptive zero-point calibration
FEETECH STS3032 bus servos for leg height and jump actuation
MPU6050 IMU for pitch, roll, and yaw sensing
AS5600 magnetic encoders on each wheel via I2C
WebSocket + JSON remote control interface (browser-based, cross-platform)
Custom PCBs: main controller, encoder, IMU, and servo debug boards
3D-printable and CNC-machinable mechanical parts with open-source design files
publicApiSurface: SMS_STS::SyncWritePosEx(u8 ID[], u8 IDN, s16 Position[], u16 Speed[], u8 ACC[]) — synchronous multi-servo position command
WiFi_SetAP() — configure ESP32 as a WiFi access point
set_sta_wifi() — connect ESP32 to an existing WiFi network
leg_loop() / jump_loop() — leg servo actuation and jump sequence
web_loop() — process WebServer and WebSocket events
bat_check() — battery voltage ADC sampling and LED indicator
Commander channels: A=pid_angle, B=pid_gyro, C=pid_distance, D=pid_speed, E=pid_yaw_angle, F=pid_yaw_gyro, G=lpf_joyy, H=pid_lqr_u, I=pid_zeropoint, J=lpf_zeropoint, K=pid_roll_angle, L=lpf_roll
WebSocket JSON fields: mode, dir (forward/back/left/right/stop/jump), height, roll, linear, angular, stable, joy_x, joy_y
Wrobot struct: height, roll, linear, angular, dir, dir_last, joyy, joyy_last, joyx, joyx_last, go
QR_State_t enum: FORWARD, BACK, RIGHT, LEFT, STOP, JUMP
